title: <div> * Clear Communication: Explain why you're implementing these systems. Emph
content:
* Clear Communication: Explain why you're implementing these systems. Emphasize that it's to make their jobs easier, reduce frustration, and help the company grow.
* Training and Onboarding: Don't just send out a memo. Walk people through the new processes, answer questions, and provide written instructions.
* Lead by Example: You and your father must consistently use these systems. If you revert to old habits, your team will too.
* Accountability: Gently reinforce the new processes. "Hey, please submit that request through the form so we don't lose track of it."
* Regular Review: After a month, sit down with your father and key employees. What's working? What's not? What needs to be adjusted?
By implementing these systems, you'll gain significant control over Majestic Design's operations, free up your father to focus on growth and client relations, and ultimately transform your company from a half-million to a million-dollar enterprise and beyond. Good luck!
context: From document: OK, I have been taken in to my father‘s company, Majestic design. It’s… — chunk 22 (chunk 22/22)
